title: Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib
tagline: Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib — spiritual town of Punjab

overview.short: Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib is situated in the village of Naulakha, Fatehgarh Sahib district in Punjab, India.
overview.description: Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib is situated in the village of Naulakha, Fatehgarh Sahib district in Punjab, India. This village is situated on Patiala-Sirhind Road. Almost 19 km from Patiala and 13 km from Sirhind.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Abhipur
topPlaces.description: Abhipur is a village and municipal council in Mohali district in state of Punjab, near Chandigarh. It is the location of Indo Global Colleges.

topPlaces.name: Naulakha, Punjab
topPlaces.description: Naulakha is a major village in Fatehgarh Sahib District, in the Indian state of Punjab. It is situated on Sirhind-Patiala road, 13 km from Sirhind and 19 km from Patiala.

topPlaces.name: Pandrali
topPlaces.description: Pandrali, sometimes spelled as Pandraali is a village in the Sirhind Block of Fatehgarh Sahib district in Punjab, India.

topPlaces.name: Jakhwali
topPlaces.description: Jakhwali, sometimes spelled as Jakhwaali is a village in the Sirhind Block of Fatehgarh Sahib district in Punjab, India.

topPlaces.name: Panjola
topPlaces.description: Panjola is a village in the Sirhind Block of Fatehgarh Sahib district in Punjab, India.

topPlaces.name: Bhatt Majra, Fatehgarh Sahib
topPlaces.description: Bhatt Majra is a village in Sirhind mandal, district Fatehgarh Sahib, state Punjab, India. The village is on the world map for organic farming, visited by Prince Charles in 2005.

topPlaces.name: Sarai Banjara train disaster
topPlaces.description: The Sarai Banjara rail disaster occurred on 2 December 2000, when a derailed freight train crossed onto the opposite track early in the morning in Punjab, India. A Howrah–Amritsar Express a passenger train coming from other direction hit the freight train head-on at speed, killing 46 people and injuring at least 150.

topPlaces.name: Sirhind
topPlaces.description: Sirhind is a twin city of Fatehgarh Sahib in Punjab, India. It is hosts the municipal council of Fatehgarh Sahib district.

items.note: Abhipur is a village and municipal council in Mohali district in state of Punjab, near Chandigarh. It is the location of Indo Global Colleges.
Naulakha is a major village in Fatehgarh Sahib District, in the Indian state of Punjab. It is situated on Sirhind-Patiala road, 13 km from Sirhind and 19 km from Patiala.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Sarai Banjara train disaster & around

items.note: The Sarai Banjara rail disaster occurred on 2 December 2000, when a derailed freight train crossed onto the opposite track early in the morning in Punjab, India. A Howrah–Amritsar Express a passenger train coming from other direction hit the freight train head-on at speed, killing 46 people and injuring at least 150.
Sirhind is a twin city of Fatehgarh Sahib in Punjab, India. It is hosts the municipal council of Fatehgarh Sahib district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (43 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurudwara Naulakha Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Abhipur, Naulakha, Punjab, Pandrali, Jakhwali, Panjola.
